name: Cosworth DFV 3.0 V8 1978
notes: Avaliado por força relativa à própria era; motor histórico, versátil, confiável e amplamente dominante no contexto clássico.

name: TAG Porsche TTE PO1 1.5 V6 Turbo 1984
notes: Conjunto turbo extremamente competitivo, forte em corrida e mais equilibrado que muitos rivais da era turbo.

name: Honda RA166E 1.5 V6 Turbo 1986
notes: Potência e classificação excepcionais, com maior risco térmico e entrega mais agressiva.

name: Renault EF15B 1.5 V6 Turbo 1986
notes: Muito forte em potência e classificação, mas com risco térmico e confiabilidade inferiores aos motores mais equilibrados.

name: Honda RA167E 1.5 V6 Turbo 1987
notes: Evolução mais madura do turbo Honda, ainda brutal em potência, com confiabilidade melhor.

name: Honda RA168E 1.5 V6 Turbo 1988
notes: The ultimate 80s turbo. Dominated 1988 with McLaren.

name: Renault RS4 3.5 V10 1992
notes: The V10 that powered the dominant Williams FW14B.

name: Renault RS5 3.5 V10 1993
notes: Evolution of the RS4, powered the FW15C.

name: Ford Zetec-R 3.5 V8 1994
notes: Powered Schumacher's first title, though lacked pure power against Renault.

name: Renault RS7 3.0 V10 1995
notes: Powered both Benetton and Williams in 1995.

name: Ferrari Tipo 051 3.0 V10 2002
notes: The heart of the dominant F2002. Not the absolute most powerful, but the best overall package.

name: BMW P83 3.0 V10 2003
notes: Motor extremamente potente, referência em velocidade de reta, mas com pacote geral menos completo e confiabilidade inferior aos melhores conjuntos dominantes.

name: Ferrari Tipo 053 3.0 V10 2004
notes: Powered the F2004. The peak of the V10 era.

name: Honda RA005E 3.0 V10 2005
notes: Forte em potência, mas menos completo e menos confiável que os melhores V10.

name: Mercedes FO110R 3.0 V10 2005
notes: Incredibly fast but very fragile, costing Raikkonen the 2005 title.

name: Renault RS25 3.0 V10 2005
notes: Famous for its drivability and traction, masking a top speed deficit.

name: Renault RS26 2.4 V8 2006
notes: First of the V8s, powered Alonso's second title.

name: Ferrari 056 2.4 V8 2007
notes: Powered Kimi to the 2007 title.

name: Mercedes FO108V 2.4 V8 2008
notes: The benchmark V8 engine, later frozen for the V8 era.

name: Mercedes FO108W 2.4 V8 2009
notes: Powered Brawn GP. Widely considered the best V8.

name: Renault RS27 2.4 V8 2011
notes: Perfectly optimized for exhaust blowing.

name: Renault RS27 2.4 V8 2013
notes: The peak of off-throttle exhaust blowing.

name: Mercedes PU106A Hybrid 2014
notes: The most dominant engine relative to the field in modern history.

name: Mercedes M11 EQ Performance 2020
notes: The ultimate evolution of the Mercedes hybrid.

name: Honda RA621H 1.6 V6 Turbo Hybrid 2021
notes: The engine that finally fought Mercedes on equal terms.

name: Ferrari 066/7 1.6 V6 Turbo Hybrid 2022
notes: Incredibly fast but structurally flawed in its first year.

name: RBPT / Honda H001 1.6 V6 t
notes: Powered the start of the Red Bull ground effect dominance.

name: Honda RBPT 1.6 V6 Turbo Hybrid 2023
notes: The benchmark of the current era in race trim.

name: Mercedes M15 1.6 V6 Turbo Hybrid 2024
notes: Powered the McLaren MCL38 resurgence.
